entry.title: How SAP Business AI looks like in Cloud ERP
entry.content:
As a Customer Success Manager at SAP for Cloud ERP, I have a front-row seat to something that keeps surprising me: the moment customers realize that AI is not a feature to switch on but a transformation to lean into.

It started at our Customer Success Day for Cloud ERP, where I presented SAP Business AI sessions (since 2024) covering the announcement of SAP Business AI and its first wave of scenarios. The energy in the room told me this conversation was far from over. So here I am, continuing it.

In this post, I want to walk you through three things:

  1. AI use cases embedded within real business processes
  2. What SAP Business AI offers
  3. SAP's direction on AI Agents — and why it matters now

 

SAP's AI Vision: The Three Pillars That Make It Real

You have probably heard this from analysts, consultants, and technology leaders alike: AI is only as good as the data and processes it runs on. SAP has built its entire AI strategy around that truth, and anchored it in three interdependent pillars.

  • Applications — SAP's end-to-end business suite connects every corner of an organization. Every transaction — in finance, procurement, logistics, or HR — originates from a single integrated framework. Processes like order-to-cash or procure-to-pay are not just digitized; they are AI-ready by design.
  • Unmatched Data — Through SAP Business Data Cloud, information from both SAP and non-SAP sources is harmonized into a unified layer. AI agents receive a 360-degree view of the business, and no time wasted reconciling conflicting definitions, no gaps in context. Data is instantly understood in its business meaning.
  • Unrivaled AI — Bring the right data to specialized agents orchestrated by Joule, and you unlock continuous optimization across every line of business. This is not isolated automation — it is an interconnected system that learns, adapts, and scales.

These three pillars create a self-reinforcing cycle of innovation. Applications feed clean processes. Clean processes feed trusted data. Trusted data powers AI. And AI, in turn, continuously improves the applications. That is the engine behind the SAP Business Suite.

We Are Already Living With AI — Are We Getting the Most From It?

Think about your own workday. You rely on AI to suggest the right words in an email. To generate a meeting summary. To autocomplete a search query. To recommend how to handle a support ticket. We have come to depend on AI assistance as a natural part of our workflow, and that is not a criticism. It is a signal.

But here is the honest reality: most of us are enabling one feature here, one capability there. And isolated AI features do not deliver the step-change efficiency that businesses need. The real value emerges when AI is embedded in an end-to-end process, not just at individual touchpoints.

What End-to-End AI Looks Like in Practice

To make this concrete, consider how an AI-enabled workflow responds to a critical business disruption, from detection all the way through to resolution. Here is an example scenario that SAP Situation Handling, combined with Joule, is designed to support:

  1.   Detect — Critical business issues are identified automatically, without waiting for a human to notice.
  2.   Alert — Relevant users are notified through SAP Fiori Launchpad, email, or SAP Mobile Start — the right person, at the right moment, in the right channel.
  3.   Navigate — Using Joule, users can surface related issues on demand, access additional context, and navigate directly to the impacted application.
  4.   Contextualize — Relevant business data is presented to support a fully informed decision.
  5.   Resolve — Generative AI provides tailored resolution recommendations, grounded in the actual business context — not generic suggestions.

No siloed dashboards. No manual correlation. No guesswork. This is what AI-assisted, process-integrated intelligence looks like in practice.

 

The Four Building Blocks of SAP Business AI

When my customer asks me:  What are the AI features/scenarios that I can activate now? 

Here is my answer to them:  What would you like to achieve with AI?

Let me break it down why. To understand where AI is embedded across SAP's Cloud ERP portfolio, it helps to think in lego building blocks(archetypes).  Each represents a distinct way AI adds value, and together they provide a consistent, scalable model for embedding intelligence across domains: finance, sales, procurement, logistics, manufacturing, and beyond.

  • AI for User Experience — AI embedded directly in Cloud ERP applications, generating text, producing summaries, or writing code, helping users work faster and with greater confidence. 
  • AI for Engagement — Conversational AI like Joule, embedded in the flow of work. Joule handles complex, multi-turn interactions that previously required significant human effort to resolve.
  • AI for Automation — Repetitive, rule-bound tasks — document classification, invoice matching, data processing — handled automatically, freeing teams for higher-value work.
  • AI for Insight and Decision-Making — Predictive capabilities such as demand forecasting, late payment prediction, and risk identification — paired with intelligent recommendations and next-best actions — helping organizations move from reactive to proactive.

 

 

The Next Chapter: From Joule Assistant to Joule Work/ Agent / Studio

A year ago, the conversation was about Joule as an AI assistant > a powerful copilot embedded across SAP applications. Today, that conversation has grown considerably.

The reasoning capability of AI foundation models has advanced at a pace few anticipated. Models can now work through complex, multi-step problems with human-like judgment. That capability is the foundation for AI agents : systems that do not just respond, but act. And in the enterprise, that distinction matters enormously.

SAP's strategy for AI agents is built around two commitments:

  • Best-in-class agents on standard business processes — Purpose-built, enterprise-ready agents deeply integrated with SAP's core business workflows — so customers benefit immediately without custom development.
  • An open framework for custom agents — The Joule Framework enables customers and partners to build their own agents, leveraging the same infrastructure SAP uses internally. Enterprise-grade capability, democratized.

The goal is not to replace human judgment. It is to put the right intelligence in the right place, at the right moment so that the people who run these businesses can focus on the decisions that truly require them.

Closing Thoughts

SAP Business AI is an architectural shift in how enterprise software works. AI is no longer bolted on after the fact. It is woven into the fabric of every process, every transaction, every interaction. With the latest announcement of "Autonomous Enterprise" at SAPPHIRE 2026, SAP is ready to help businesses transform how they operate.

The organizations that gain the most will not be those that activate the most features. They will be the ones who think end-to-end "with the business outcome in mind" and let AI do the same.

 

I would love to hear how your organization is approaching SAP Business AI adoption. Are you starting with a specific archetype? Exploring AI Agents? Working through a business case internally? Share your thoughts and questions in the comments below.

#SAPBusinessAI  #Joule  #AIAgents  #CloudERP  #GenerativeAI  #SAPCommunity

entry.title: Principal Propagation from SAP Build Process Automation workflow to S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

When building automated workflows in SAP Build Process Automation, a common requirement is to call backend APIs in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud on behalf of the user who triggered the workflow, not a generic technical user. This is where principal propagation becomes essential.
Principal propagation ensures that the identity of the originating user is securely forwarded through the call chain, allowing S/4HANA to enforce its own authorization checks based on who actually initiated the action. Without it, all requests reach the backend under a shared service account, which undermines auditability, segregation of duties, and fine-grained access control.
In this article, I walk through the exact configuration steps I followed to enable principal propagation between SAP Build Process Automation and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, covering the trust setup on SAP BTP, the destination configuration, and the corresponding communication arrangement on the S/4HANA side. If you are facing the same challenge, this guide should give you a clear, step-by-step path to get it working.

Prerequisites

Before you begin, make sure you have:
  • An SAP BTP subaccount with SAP Build Process Automation enabled
  • Access to your SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system with an administrator role
  • An existing workflow in SAP Build Process Automation that needs to call an S/4HANA API

Step 1 — Configure S/4HANA Public Cloud

1.1 Create a Communication User

In the SAP S/4HANA Fiori launchpad, navigate to Communication Management > Maintain Communication Users and create a dedicated user for inbound communication. Make sure you save the password; this will be needed in configuring the Destination.

1.2 Create a Communication Scenario

Navigate to Communication Management > Communication Scenarios and either select an existing scenario that exposes the API you need or create a custom one. Note the scenario ID — you will need it in the next step.
Regardless of whether you use an existing or custom scenario, make sure that OAuth 2.0 is enabled as a supported authorization method under the inbound communication settings of the scenario. This is a prerequisite for the SAML bearer assertion flow to work - if OAuth 2.0 is not listed as an authorization option in the scenario, the Communication Arrangement will not allow you to configure it, and principal propagation will not be possible for that API.

1.3 Create a Communication System

Before creating the Communication System in S/4HANA, you first need to export the trust certificate from BTP.
 
In the SAP BTP Cockpit, navigate to Connectivity > Destinations, scroll down to the Destination Trust section, and click Export to download the certificate file. The file will be in .pem format — keep it ready for the next step.
 
In your SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system, navigate to Communication Management > Communication Systems and create a new system. Set the Host field to your BTP subaccount subdomain.
 
Under the OAuth 2.0 Settings section, enable OAuth 2.0 Identity Provider and upload the .pem file you just exported from BTP. Once uploaded, the certificate details will be displayed — locate the CN (Common Name) field, copy its value, and paste it into the OAuth 2.0 SAML Issuer field.
 
This links S/4HANA's trust configuration to your BTP subaccount and is what allows S/4HANA to validate the SAML assertions that BTP will generate during principal propagation.
 

 

1.4 Create a Communication Arrangement

Navigate to Communication Management > Communication Arrangements, create a new arrangement, and link it to the communication scenario, communication system, and communication user created in the previous steps.
 
Note - User for inbound communication must be added with OAuth 2.0 Authentication Method. 

After save, you can see the OAuth 2.0 Details. These will be used in setting up the Destination in BTP.

Step 2 — Configure the BTP Destination

In your SAP BTP cockpit, navigate to your subaccount and open Connectivity > Destinations. Create a new destination with the following key settings:
 
Property Value
AuthenticationOAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion
AudienceYour S/4HANA system URL
Client KeyCommunication user name
Token Service URLOAuth token endpoint from the Communication Arrangement
Token Service URL Type DedicatedDedicated
Use Basic Credentials for Token Service✔️
Token Service UserCommunication user name
Token Service PasswordPassword for the Communication User created in Step 1.1
AuthnContextClassRef
urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:ac:classes:PreviousSession
Name Id Format
urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:emailAddress
User Id Source
email
Additional Properties
 
compactAssertion
true
 

 

 

Step 3 — Configure SAP Build Process Automation

Open your workflow in SAP Build Process Automation and locate the action step that calls the S/4HANA API.
In the step configuration, enable the option "Run on behalf of" and map it to the workflow initiator or the relevant user identity. This tells SBPA to pass the user context when invoking the destination, which, combined with the OAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion destination and the SAML trust in S/4HANA, completes the propagation chain.

How It All Fits Together

Once configured end-to-end, the flow works as follows:
  1. A user triggers the workflow in SAP Build Process Automation
  2. The action step runs on behalf of that user and calls the BTP destination
  3. BTP generates a SAML assertion carrying the user identity and exchanges it for an OAuth token from S/4HANA
  4. S/4HANA validates the token against the Communication System trust and applies its own authorization checks for that specific user

Conclusion

Principal propagation is a few configuration steps across systems, but each piece is critical. Missing any one of them, such as not uploading the correct signature in the Communication System, will cause the setup to fail entirely.
 
If you run into issues, the most useful debugging step is to test the destination directly from the BTP cockpit using Check Connection and verify in the S/4HANA application log which user identity is being received.
 
I hope this guide saves you the trial and error. Feel free to drop questions in the comments.

entry.title: Frictionless Payments: How United TranzActions Is Redefining the Invoice-to-Cash Cycle
entry.content:
From Mark Tapia (United TranzActions) and Stephan Müller (SAP)

For many SAP finance and accounts receivable teams, digital payment processing exists in an awkward space: it is essential to operations, yet often not truly integrated into the SAP Cloud ERP environment.

Teams frequently switch between multiple systems to process and manage digital payments. They manually enter remittance data, investigate returned ACH payments, and absorb rising credit card fees that quietly erode margins. While the underlying SAP data is clean and authoritative, the payment layer for credit card or other wallet payments surrounding the SAP Cloud ERP system often remains fragmented and disconnected

But it does not have to be this way.

United TranzActions (UTA) was built to solve exactly this challenge and is now fully integrated with the SAP digital payments add-on  (DPA). UTA is live as a certified Payment Service Provider (PSP) for SAP Cloud ERP and SAP S/4HANA customers, giving SAP enterprises a path to true end-to-end payment automation without leaving their SAP environment

 

What Is the  SAP digital payments add-on 

 The SAP Digital Payments Add-On is SAP’s certified framework for integrating third-party payment service providers directly into SAP Cloud ERP and SAP S/4HANA environments.

Instead of routing payment data through custom integrations, custom ABAP development, or external payment portals, the DPA provides a native bridge between SAP business processes and a certified PSP’s payment infrastructure. This enables organizations to process digital payments in a standardized, automated, and fully SAP-integrated way to process digital payments automatically end to end .

The result includes:

  • Instant payment posting
  • Automated reconciliation
  • Drastically Reduced manual effort
  • Clean-core integration aligned with SAP best practices
  • A solution framework supported and maintained by SAP

UTA’s adapter is now live on the DPA framework, hosted in the US East (Virginia) data center, and available to SAP customers today.

 

The Full Payment Stack, Inside SAP

UTA’s DPA integration supports a comprehensive range of payment operations — all executed from within your SAP environment:

  • Credit card processing (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover) with authorization, pre-authorization, capture, void, and refund
  • ACH (e-check) — transfers money from your customer’s bank account directly into your company’s DDA bank account of choice.
  • Compliant surcharging, allowing merchants to offset credit card processing costs by passing fees to cardholders in full compliance with card brand rules and applicable state law
  • Level 3 data processing for B2B and government transactions, unlocking significantly lower interchange rates on qualifying card volume
  • Tokenization and card-on-file registration for recurring billing and streamlined repeat transactions
  • Multi-MID support, enabling organizations with multiple legal entities or company codes to route transactions to the appropriate Merchant ID within a single SAP environment

 

Automated Cash Application: Closing the Loop

Processing a payment is only half the equation. The other half - matching that payment to the correct open invoice, posting it to the GL, and clearing the AR line - is where most finance teams still lose hours every week.

UTA’s integration is designed to close that loop. When a payment is processed through the DPA, remittance data flows directly back into SAP, enabling automated matching against open receivables. The result is faster invoice closure, a measurable reduction in Days Sales Outstanding (DSO), and AR teams that spend their time on exceptions -not on manual posting.

For organizations running high transaction volumes across multiple company codes, this shift from manual to automated cash application can represent a significant operational improvement. UTA’s AR automation capabilities extend beyond the DPA itself, connecting payment acceptance with downstream reconciliation and reporting in a single, SAP-native workflow.

 

UTA - built for B2B Industries With Complex Payment Environments

UTA has spent over three decades working with B2B enterprises - manufacturers, distributors, building materials suppliers, trucking companies, equipment dealers, and professional services firms. These industries share a common set of payment challenges: large average ticket sizes that make interchange costs material, a mix of card and ACH payment types, and AR teams managing high volumes of invoices across multiple customers and terms.

The UTA-DPA integration was purpose-built for this environment. PCI-DSS and SOC 2 certified infrastructure handles the compliance burden, so your IT and finance teams don’t have to. U.S.-based support and a dedicated Relationship Manager are available to assist during and after the onboarding process. And because the integration runs natively inside SAP, there are no parallel systems to maintain, no duplicate data entry, and no reconciliation gap between your payment records and your ERP.

 

Getting Started

UTA is live in the SAP Partner Finder directory and available for onboarding today. SAP merchants interested in activating UTA as their PSP through the Digital Payments Add-On can reach the UTA team at:

  • Contact: Mark Tapia
  • Phone: (800) 852-5256 ext 3028
  • Email: mtapia@unitedtranzactions.com

 If your organization is running SAP Cloud ERP  or S/4HANA and evaluating ways to modernize your payment and AR operations, UTA’s DPA integration is worth a close look.

entry.title: Start Small. Think at Scale. Business Professionals Can Use SAP & Claude to Build a Business Case.
entry.content:
Note: This blog provides an actionable step-by-step proof of concept (POC) for the SAP and Anthropic partnership that a business professional can build today using SAP's free Business Accelerator Hub sandbox data and Claude. The Master Prompt is included below. A sample output is provided in the Addendum. Already familiar with this context? Skip directly to The Proof of Concept section.

Build the POC. Create the Business Case. Start the Conversation.

The recent SAP and Anthropic partnership announcement ignited a new wave of opportunities. It got me thinking about how the combined capabilities of Claude and SAP will strengthen enterprises around the globe.


At the same time, I found myself wondering. How can Business Professionals add real value to what needs to happen to put these capabilities in place for maximum success?

It reminded me of a simple metaphor. Imagine digging a new swimming pool with a shovel. You are making progress. Slowly. Painfully. Then someone pulls up with a bulldozer. The hole that would have taken weeks gets done in a day. That is the uplift the SAP and Anthropic partnership represents for organizations who are ready to act.

So how can Business Professionals help jumpstart the process?

Consider the impact of creating a simple proof of concept using SAP's own sandbox data and Claude, grounded in business case best practices, to help prioritize initiatives that can create compelling value.

The Market Opportunity

The scale of this partnership is significant. SAP and Anthropic are not simply connecting two products. They are building an Autonomous Enterprise vision where Claude powered agents operate through SAP Business AI Platform, coordinate across SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Ariba, and other SAP products via Model Context Protocol (MCP), and are accessible to users through Joule.

IDC projects AI spending driven by agentic AI will reach $1.3 trillion by 2029, growing at 31.9% year over year. In a best case scenario Gartner projects a $450 billion enterprise AI opportunity by 2035. Yet according to the DSAG Investment Report published in February 2026, among SAP customers already using AI, only 3% are running SAP Business AI in production today. The opportunity is enormous. But before Business Professionals can seize it they must consider creating POCs and business cases that will move their organization from insight to action.

The New API Policy. A Quality Framework.

It is worth highlighting a recent SAP announcement that is directly relevant to this partnership. SAP's API policy governs how autonomous external agents make sequential API calls into SAP systems.

Consider this approach as a quality framework.

Think Lean Six Sigma, ITIL, or ISO 9001 for mission critical Cloud ERP processes. Controlled variation. Trusted inputs. Governed outputs.

Claude operating inside SAP's approved architecture brings these same quality principles to agentic AI. As a Six Sigma certified practitioner I can say with confidence that controlled variation and governed outputs are not optional in mission critical processes. They are the standard.

Errors in enterprise data cannot be fully eliminated but they must be managed, minimized, and controlled continuously. They stem from uncontrolled inputs, ungoverned processes, and systems operating outside defined boundaries.

SAP's framework controls that variation at the source, reducing risk to the lowest achievable level and keeping mission critical processes operating with the precision enterprises depend on.

From Insight to Action. Building Your Case

Consider this scenario. You are a Business Professional who has just learned about the SAP and Anthropic partnership. You see the opportunity clearly. You know your organization has processes that AI can benefit, especially with Claude and SAP working together.

But where do you start?

You are not a developer. You do not have a project budget approved. You cannot stand up a Joule environment this afternoon.

What you do have is business knowledge, SAP familiarity, and a growing confidence in what Claude can do. Most importantly you have a clear sense of where the pain and inefficiencies are in your organization today.

That is enough to get started.

The path forward has two parallel tracks that reinforce each other:

  1. Delivering a proof of concept (POC) that shows what is possible
  2. Creating a business case that justifies the investment to make it real at scale.

The good news is that SAP has already given you the tools to do both.

The SAP Business Accelerator Hub was built explicitly to accelerate customer time to value. Free to access. No SAP license required. No IT ticket needed. Just a free SAP ID and a clear business question you want Claude to help answer.

The proof of concept you build today becomes the evidence that funds the enterprise deployment tomorrow. The business case you build around it gives your organization's executive sponsors the confidence to act.

This approach is how Business Professionals provide meaningful leadership, collaborating with IT counterparts and key stakeholders to move from insight to action.

The Proof of Concept  

This POC is intentionally simple and uses SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition procurement data available through the SAP Business Accelerator Hub sandbox. It is a minimum viable product (MVP) approach designed to show what is possible without requiring engineering support, IT involvement, or a live SAP system. Every step is something a Business Professional can execute independently.

What you are about to build is a working example of agentic AI. Claude is not simply answering a question. It is reasoning over real SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition data, identifying risk, prioritizing action, and recommending next steps. That is agentic AI in action. And it is a preview of what runs inside Joule at enterprise scale.

The following steps walk you through the POC from start to finish.

What You Do

  1. Register for a free SAP ID at api.sap.com. Takes a few minutes.
  2. Go to the Purchase Order API (CE_PURCHASEORDER_0001) in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. This is the current OData V4 Purchase Order API for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and one example of the many sandbox APIs available across SAP's core product portfolio.
  3. Click “Try Out” at the top. Select “Sandbox” from the dropdown on the right side. Navigate to the Purchase Order section in the left hand navigation. Click the /PurchaseOrder “GET” option. Click “Run.”
  4. Copy the JSON response that appears under “Response Body.”
  5. Open Claude at claude.ai. Paste the data and enter the Master Prompt below. Note: A free Claude account is sufficient for this POC. Claude Pro provides higher usage limits if needed.

What Claude Does

  1. Reads and interprets the SAP S/4HANA Cloud purchase order data.
  2. Identifies which exceptions represent the highest business risk.
  3. Assigns a risk score to each record with a clear rationale.
  4. Recommends specific actions for each prioritized item.
  5. Produces a scored table ready for your business case conversation.
  6. Offers to draft a supplier communication for the highest risk item.

Master Prompt

Copy and paste the following prompt into Claude along with your JSON data (as noted above). You are reviewing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition purchase order data retrieved directly from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub sandbox via the Purchase Order API (CE_PURCHASEORDER_0001).

[START PROMPT]

Review the records provided and analyze the following fields: PurchaseOrder number, Supplier, CreationDate, PurchaseOrderDate, PurchasingProcessingStatus, PurchaseOrderDeletionCode, ReleaseIsNotCompleted, and PurchasingReleaseStrategy.

Based on your analysis:

  1. Identify purchase orders that require attention. Focus on deletion flagged records, incomplete releases, unusual processing statuses, and supplier concentration patterns.
  2. Assign a priority level of High, Medium, or Low to each flagged record with a brief rationale.
  3. Recommend a specific action for each flagged item.
  4. Produce a prioritized table with the following columns: PO Number, Supplier, Creation Date, Processing Status, Deletion Flag, Release Complete, Priority Level, Recommended Action.
  5. Provide a two to three sentence plain English summary of your overall findings before the table.
  6. After the table offer to draft a supplier communication for the highest priority item.
  7. Keep your total response concise. Summary, table, and offer only. No additional commentary unless asked.

[END PROMPT]

What You Just Built

A working example of agentic AI reasoning over real SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition procurement data. Built by a Business Professional. Using SAP's own tools. View the sample output in the Addendum below

This is your building block. The cornerstone of your business case. And the start of a meaningful conversation with your IT counterparts, key stakeholders, and executive sponsors about what is possible and the value this use case can deliver.

Start small. Think at scale.

From POC to Production

This POC uses the SAP Business Accelerator Hub sandbox as a starting point outside of SAP's production architecture. That is intentional. The goal is to demonstrate the concept quickly and accessibly. Free sandbox data. No infrastructure required.

Joule Studio is where developers and business teams can go next. A governed, enterprise grade environment where Business Professionals and developers collaborate to build both pre-built and custom agents tailored to their specific processes, data, and workflows. Joule Studio requires SAP BTP, a Joule subscription, SAP Build licensing, and IT provisioning to activate. It is the right next step. See Activate Joule Studio in 5 Simple Steps for full prerequisites.  

Complementary approaches. Each with its own role.

In production this same capability runs natively inside SAP Business AI Platform through the MCP Gateway. It is accessible to users through Joule. Everything you just demonstrated is the reasoning capability that sits behind the potential production implementation. The logic transfers directly.

In this POC the Hub provides everything you need to show what is possible.

Building the Business Case

A strong business case requires the right elements addressed with clarity and evidence. The POC you just built directly addresses two of the most critical. It defines the problem in concrete terms and demonstrates measurable value, ROI potential, and a clear view of what is possible.

The implementation path and risk and governance elements are addressed by SAP's approved architecture. The path runs through SAP Business AI Platform, Joule, and the production framework SAP and Anthropic have built together.

That represents a credible and governed foundation for any business case conversation.


Security and Data You Can Depend On

The same security and governance framework that protects your SAP environment today protects every action Claude takes inside it. The controls do not change because AI is involved. They extend to cover it.

Every action is governed by the same role-based access controls your organization operates under today. Every decision Claude makes is traceable through a full audit trail.

Connecting external data sources to the SAP environment requires proper integration through the SAP Integration Suite, ensuring data flows through governed, secure pathways before Claude ever touches it.

The Path Forward

The SAP and Anthropic partnership is designed for enterprise scale, grounded in trust and quality outcomes. Today, Business Professionals can create a tangible starting point and play a leadership role in the conversation with IT and business stakeholders about initiatives that can create lasting value.

SAP and Anthropic have announced plans to bring Claude to SAP Business AI Platform. Joule Studio, the development environment where agents are built and deployed, has general availability planned for later this year.

Now is the time to build your POC and business case so your organization is ready.

Take it one step at a time.

Start small. Think at scale.

Join the Conversation

Your likes and comments are welcome and appreciated.

  • Are you excited about the SAP and Anthropic partnership?
  • Were you aware of the SAP Business Accelerator Hub?
  • What are you working on with Claude or your favorite Assistant AI that can be applied to the Autonomous Enterprise vision?

About the Author

Rich Blumberg is an award-winning writer and author of two Amazon published books, Novi’s Journey to the Magic Garden and Job Seeking Warriors – A Mentors Guide to Winning. He's the President of World Sales Solutions, LLC, (WSS) a management consulting, low-code, no-code, and AI services firm. For more than 20 years he has worked as an SAP consultant partnering with SAP and their ecosystem organizations around the globe. Outside of work Rich serves as a Drexel University Alumni Board of Governors Emeritus, an ENGin English tutor supporting Ukrainian citizens, and an aspiring guitar player.

Related Blogs

  • How the AI Content Lifecycle Turns Complexity into Clarity at Scale(Part 2)
  • AI-Powered Knowledge Gap Analysis and Content Lifecycle Governance(Part 1)
  • You Can Build an Agentic AI Workflow. No Coding, No Engineering, No Problem.
  • AI at Warp Speed: Lessons from the Horse and Buggy Collapse

Addendum SAP S/4HANA Cloud POC Output

Purchase Order Analysis using the SAP Business Accelerator Hub Sandbox and Claude

Summary

Of the 50 purchase orders reviewed, 6 require immediate attention. Two records carry both a deletion flag and an incomplete release status, representing the highest risk. Four additional records show a deletion code with a completed processing status, suggesting they were cancelled but may warrant confirmation. Supplier 17300001 dominates the dataset with over 60% concentration, which itself warrants a supplier risk review.

Note: In a production environment this same analysis can run across thousands of purchase orders in seconds.

Prioritized Purchase Order Exception Table

Draft Supplier Communication

Highest Priority Item: Purchase Orders 4500000021 and 4500000022 | Supplier USSU-VSF01

Subject: Purchase Orders 4500000021 and 4500000022 – Urgent Status Clarification Required

Dear USSU-VSF01 Team,

I am writing regarding two purchase orders currently showing an unresolved status in our SAP S/4HANA system that require your immediate attention.

Purchase Orders 4500000021 and 4500000022, both dated August 18, 2016, are currently flagged for deletion while the approval release process remains incomplete. This combination of statuses prevents us from properly closing or reactivating these orders and creates a compliance risk in our procurement records.

We kindly request that you confirm the following by end of business this week:

  1. Whether any goods or services were delivered against either purchase order.
  2. Whether any outstanding invoices or payment obligations remain.
  3. Your agreement to formally close both orders if no outstanding obligations exist.

Please contact our procurement team at your earliest convenience so we can resolve this matter and ensure our records accurately reflect the current state of our relationship.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this request.

Best regards,
Procurement Team
Company Code 1710

Data source: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Purchase Order API (CE_PURCHASEORDER_0001) via SAP Business Accelerator Hub Sandbox. Analysis performed by

entry.title: Managing Media Production Services Projects with S/4HANA Cloud - Part 2
entry.content:
In Part 1 we structured project VFX4712, staffed the team, and reviewed commercial billing terms. Now we close the loop: confirm time and shot completions, analyse real-time project margins, and generate billing requests — all within a single integrated system, with SAP Joule and scheduling agents available at every critical step.

Once a project is underway, two parallel workstreams drive the billing cycle for a media production company: time confirmation by VFX artists and designers, and shot completion confirmation from production systems. Both must be accurate before billing requests can be raised. SAP S/4HANA Cloud provides purpose-built tools for each, with SAP Joule and scheduling agents available to eliminate manual bottlenecks and ensure nothing is missed before the billing window closes.This post walks the full execution phase — from chasing missing timesheet entries, through posting direct activity allocations for confirmed shots, to real-time margin analysis and finally preparing project billing requests ready for invoicing.

Section 01: App Notify Missing Time

Ensuring Every Billable Hour Is Captured

Before any billing run begins, project managers must verify that all billable hours are recorded and approved. The Notify Missing Time app delivers a filterable, project-wide view of planned effort versus recorded and approved hours for every team member across every work package.

The view surfaces gaps at a glance. In the Asset Creation work package, the staffed resource has recorded 59 of his planned 67 hours (12% missing), while another resource shows 30 hours recorded against a planned 27 —  an over-recording requiring manager review. 

  • Send Reminder — dispatches a notification to the selected worker prompting them to record and submit hours before the billing due date closes.
  • Edit Timesheet on behalf — the project manager can enter time directly for a team member who is unavailable, ensuring no billable effort is lost from the current period.

Why this matters for billing: In usage-based VFX billing, missing time entries prevent accurate cost accumulation and block the generation of revenue recognition documents. Clearing this queue before the billing window closes is a mandatory prerequisite for a clean billing run.

Section 02: SAP Joule AI Assisted

AI-Assisted Time Monitoring on Mobile with SAP Joule

For project managers on the move, SAP Joule provides a conversational AI interface accessible on mobile devices. Rather than navigating multiple screens and applying filters, the manager simply asks Joule for a summary of missing time recordings and receives an actionable breakdown in seconds.

Conversational insight, anywhere

In the example shown, the manager asks Joule to "Show missing time records" for the current month. Joule queries the system and responds with a precise, actionable summary for the period 1–31 May 2026:

Project Conversational Insights

 

  • 53 workers did not record time
  • 32 workers did not submit time
  • 1 time recording is pending approval

Joule then offers drill-down quick actions — Hours not recorded or Hours not submitted — enabling the manager to act on each category immediately without switching to a desktop browser.

 
Automation opportunity: This same Joule capability can be wired into a scheduling agent that monitors billing due dates across all projects and proactively dispatches reminders as deadlines approach — removing the need for manual review and ensuring billing completeness at scale.
 

Section 03 - App: Manage Direct Activity Allocation

Confirming Shot Completions from Production Systems

Completed shots — the unit of measurement for usage-based VFX billing — are posted through Direct Activity Allocation documents. In a production environment these are typically interfaced automatically from external shot-tracking or production management tools via standard APIs. In this walkthrough the entry was made manually to illustrate the posting flow and its downstream effects.

Real-time revenue recognition: The RR (Rev Rec Document) — entry 7100205091 — is generated automatically the instant the shot completion is posted. Recognised revenue and project margin are updated in real time, with no end-of-month batch processing required. Shot completions flow directly into the Project Financial Booklet as soon as they are confirmed.

Section 04 App:  Project Financial Booklet - Professional Services

Real-Time Project Margin at a Glance

With shot completions posted and time confirmed, project margins are immediately visible in the Project Financial Booklet. This app provides a comprehensive financial overview for one or multiple projects simultaneously, organised across configurable booklets with Auto-Refresh enabled for live data.

Project Margin Overview — VFX Services for Neon / 50 ShotsLayout Builder — configurable KPI columns & ad-hoc calculations

The recognised margin of 63,384.21 EUR reflects revenue already recognised through posted shot completions and time entries. The cost base will converge toward the planned baseline as the remaining work packages are charged across the project lifecycle.

The booklet navigation exposes four built-in views, and the layout builder reveals the full library of configurable measures:

  • Project Margin Overview — recognised vs planned revenue, cost and margin; supports single-project or cross-portfolio views
  • Project Billing Element View — billed and unbilled amounts per billing element, aligned to sales order line items
  • Project Hour Details — staffed versus recorded effort at work package and resource level
  • Profit Center View — cross-project margin monitoring for practice or portfolio leads managing multiple simultaneous productions

Ad-hoc calculated columns: Beyond the standard KPI library — which includes accrued revenue, deferred revenue, EAC planned quantities, imminent loss reserves, and margin percentage — the layout builder supports custom formulas. Teams can add project-specific metrics such as blended realisation rate or per-shot cost by tier without IT involvement. 

Section 05 - App: Manage Project Billing

Which Services Are Ready to Bill?

The Manage Project Billing app consolidates all billing elements, showing planned revenue, amounts in process, estimated actuals from posted transactions, and the live To Bill amount for each element. An in-app guided tour walks first-time users through the billing request creation flow end to end.

Estimated actuals are calculated in real time from confirmed activity allocations and posted time entries For usage-based elements, the amount is the confirmed shot quantity multiplied by the contracted unit price. For fixed-price elements, it reflects the contractual milestone amount due at the billing date.

Billing element VFX4712.0.6 already has a request in progress (S000003679),  The system prevents duplicate requests from being created for the same element while one is pending review. Remaining elements with non-zero To Bill amounts are ready for the Prepare Billing action.

Scheduling agents: The billing preparation step can be fully automated. A scheduling agent monitors billing due dates, identifies elements ready to bill, and pushes them into a human review queue — notified on the SAP Fiori Launchpad or SAP Start mobile app — without requiring the project manager to check the billing screen manually.

Section 06 - App Prepare Billing | Human-in-the-loop

Generating the Project Billing Request

A New Project Billing Request (S000003679) has been created for billing element VFX4712.0.6 — a usage-based time-and-expenses work package with an estimated actual of 22,000 EUR and a billable quantity of 1 PC. The human-in-the-loop approach demonstrated here gives finance teams full control to review, adjust, postpone, or write off individual usage items before the request proceeds to invoicing.

 

  • Source document: Journal entry 2300004836 — Periodic Service (P003), with Shot Grid Ref: 560… providing full traceability back to the originating external production system
  • Disposition options: Each usage line can be individually flagged as To Bill, To Postpone, or To Write Off, giving finance teams granular control over what enters the invoice document
  • Consistency check: The Check Consistency action validates the request against the sales order, project structure, and profit centre assignments before submission, preventing downstream invoicing errors
  • Service date captured: 18.05.2026 — ensuring correct period assignment on the resulting invoice document for accurate revenue period reporting

End-to-end traceability: The Shot Grid reference embedded in the journal entry note field creates an unbroken audit trail from the external production system through to the final customer invoice — essential for dispute resolution in complex multi-delivery VFX contracts.

Section 07 - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Home | SAP Start

Task Notifications: Staying on Top of the Billing Cycle

Once billing requests are prepared — whether initiated manually or pushed by a scheduling agent — the responsible approvers are notified directly on the SAP Fiori Launchpad under To-Dos. The same tasks are accessible on mobile devices via the SAP Start app, ensuring no billing cycle action is missed regardless of where the approver is working.

What Would You Like to See Next?

We have now walked the complete end-to-end journey for a VFX services project: from initial project setup and staffing, through commercial billing terms, time and shot confirmation, real-time margin analysis, and generating billing requests. The remaining steps — standard invoice document generation, output management, and incoming payment posting — follow the well-established Cloud ERP patterns that most SAP professionals will be familiar with and are not covered here.

The media and entertainment industry has a rich set of further scenarios worth exploring in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.  Let us know in the comments which ones matter most to your organisation.

If you found this walkthrough useful, share it with your SAP network and drop a comment below with the topic you would like covered next.

entry.title: Managing Media Production Services Projects with S/4HANA Cloud - Part 1
entry.content:
Media production companies — particularly those delivering visual effects (VFX), animation, and post-production services — operate in a uniquely complex commercial environment. A single project may involve fixed-price deliverables such as asset creation and onsite supervision, alongside usage-based services where billing is driven by the volume of shots completed at each complexity tier.

Traditional ERP systems struggle to accommodate this duality. Project managers are forced to reconcile billing models across disconnected tools, time recording is inconsistent, and external shot completion data from editing systems arrives with no structured integration path.

This blog demonstrates how SAP S/4HANA Cloud — specifically its Customer Project Management capabilities — addresses these challenges end-to-end. We walk through a real-world example: VFX Services for Neon / 50 Shots, a project serving Electronics, INC. (DE), covering everything from project planning through billing readiness.

Section 1

Structuring a VFX Project with Mixed Billing Models

Work packages map directly to billing elements, enabling simultaneous fixed-price and usage-based contract terms within a single project.

When project VFX4712 is created for a 50-shot engagement, the project manager defines six work packages — each corresponding to a distinct commercial commitment. SAP S/4HANA Cloud enforces a deliberate separation between how work is planned and tracked internally and how it is billed externally.

 

VFX Tier 1 – Simple (work package VFX4712.1.4) illustrates the usage-based model clearly: 20 planned pieces (=Shots) with a planned revenue of 56,000 EUR. Each shot approved by the editorial system increments the billable quantity — the project manager does not need to manually count or reconcile. Similarly, VFX Tier 3 – Complex carries the highest planned revenue at 138,000 EUR for 12 shots, reflecting the premium pricing for complex visual work.

In contrast, Asset Creation (VFX4712.1.1) carries a fixed planned revenue of 45,000 EUR regardless of how many hours are consumed internally. The separation ensures revenue recognition follows the correct accounting rules for each contract type

The Planning tab shows all six work packages with their cost vs. effort actuals, planned quantity (service-based), and planned revenue per billing element. Recognized revenue of 14,888 EUR against 7,404 EUR actual cost reflects a 50.27% margin at this stage of the project.

 

Key Design Principle

SAP S/4HANA Cloud allows work packages to carry both an internal cost structure (planned effort, staffed roles, purchasing) and an external billing structure (billing element, contract type, planned revenue) simultaneously. This eliminates the need for a separate billing spreadsheet and ensures financial reporting is always aligned with project execution.

Section 02

Integrated Resource Management: Connecting People to Work

Resource requests are raised directly on work packages, giving project managers a real-time view of who is committed to which deliverable — and how much capacity remains unstaffed.

With the project structure in place, the next step is staffing. SAP S/4HANA Cloud's integrated resource management surfaces 17 resource requests across the project's work packages — each request specifying a role grade, time window, and required effort in hours.

For example, Asset Creation work package, three consultants are engaged across three grade levels. The staffing view confirms that requested and staffed efforts are fully aligned — a green signal for the project manager that no capacity gap exists on this work package.

 

 
 
 
  • Role-grade costing: Each resource request carries a grade (T001–T003) which drives internal cost rates — enabling accurate margin simulation before work begins.
  • Cross-package visibility: The same individual can be staffed across multiple work packages, and the system enforces utilization limits to prevent over-commitment.
  • Billing relevance: On time-and-material billing elements, the staffed role grade determines the billing rate applied when time is recorded — creating a direct link from staffing to invoice.

Section 03

Ensuring Every Billable Hour is Captured Before Billing

The Time Recording view compares staffed effort against recorded effort per worker, surfacing gaps that must be resolved before billing can be initiated.

As the project progresses, VFX artists and designers record their hours against specific work packages. SAP S/4HANA Cloud continuously compares staffed effort (committed capacity) against recorded effort (actual time entries submitted), and calculates the delta as Missing Hours — visible to the project manager at any point.

In the current snapshot, the Asset Creation work package shows mixed compliance. 

Critically, VFX Supervision shows zero hours recorded ,despite a combined staffed commitment of 94h. With a billing due date approaching on 22.05.2026, the project manager must act immediately to send reminders and ensure time entries are submitted before the billing run is triggered.

 

Operational Insight: Protecting Revenue Before Billing

The “Missing Hours” column is the project manager’s primary control mechanism before initiating a billing run. On T&E billing elements, unrecorded hours directly translate to lost revenue. SAP S/4HANA Cloud allows the project manager to send targeted reminders to individual workers from this view — ensuring 100% time recording compliance before the invoice is generated. With the latest release, project managers get AI-assisted features that take agentic approach to inform the missing hours and send reminders.

Section 04

Commercial Billing Terms and Upcoming Due Dates

The Billing tab consolidates all six billing elements — their contract types, due dates, and amounts to bill — providing a single source of truth for commercial obligations.

Once project execution is underway, the Billing tab becomes the commercial control center. For project VFX4712, six billing elements have been configured — each derived from its corresponding work package and carrying its own contract type, billing due date, and amount.

 
Billing Element Description Contract Type Billing Due Date To Bill (EUR)
VFX4712.0.1Asset CreationFixed Price18.05.202615,000.00
VFX4712.0.2VFX Onsite SupervisionFixed Price22.05.20260.00
VFX4712.0.3VFX Tier 1 – SimpleUsage-Based18.05.20260.00
VFX4712.0.4VFX Tier 2 – ModerateUsage-Based—0.00
VFX4712.0.5Project-Based Service: T&EUsage-Based—0.00
VFX4712.0.6Project-Based Service: T&EUsage-Based—0.00

The Asset Creation billing element (VFX4712.0.1) has a due date of 18.05.2026 with 15,000 EUR ready to bill — the first billing milestone approaching imminently. The VFX Tier 1 – Simple usage-based element also carries a due date of 18.05.2026, but currently shows 0.00 EUR to bill, reflecting that shot completions have not yet been confirmed by the external editing system.

The Billing Documents section below the elements shows no invoices have been generated yet. This is intentional — the project team is ensuring time recording completeness (Section 03) and shot confirmation updates are processed before initiating any billing documents.

  • Billing due date monitoring: Due dates on billing elements drive automated reminders and billing run scheduling — the system ensures no milestone is missed.
  • Contract type enforcement: Fixed Price elements bill on date or milestone trigger; Usage-Based elements accumulate quantities from shot confirmation events before billing can proceed.
  • Profit center assignment: All six elements are assigned to Consulting Unit A (YB101), ensuring revenue recognition flows to the correct organizational unit automatically.
With latest release,  AI-assisted project services features enable agentic approach to monitoring billing due dates and resolving action with human-in-loop approach. more details here. SAP AI Assisted project services 

Coming Up in Part 2: Initiating the Billing Run - Part 2 

With the project structure defined, resources staffed, time recording reviewed, and billing terms confirmed, the project is ready for its first billing cycle. In Part 2, we will demonstrate how shot completion updates from external editing systems flow into SAP S/4HANA Cloud, how usage quantities are confirmed on usage-based billing elements, and how the billing run generates customer invoices for both Fixed Price and Usage-Based elements simultaneously — completing the revenue cycle.

entry.title: A Missing Distinction in PO Output: Why Changed Purchase Orders Deserve Their Own Email Template
entry.content:
Clear communication with suppliers is a critical success factor in procurement processes. In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, email output plays a central role in informing suppliers about new Purchase Orders as well as subsequent changes.

SAP provides the app Maintain Email Templates, which allows to standardize and streamline email communication. While this functionality is highly valuable, a closer look at Purchase Order output reveals a gap that can lead to confusion on the supplier side.

At the moment, the same predelivered email template is used for both new Purchase Orders and changed Purchase Orders. This blog post explains why this is problematic, outlines a proposed enhancement, and highlights the business benefits of introducing a dedicated email template for PO changes.

The Current Situation

In the app Maintain Email Templates (F1306A), users can create custom email templates by copying predelivered ones.

For Purchase Orders, SAP currently delivers only one standard template: “Purchase Order Default Email Template” (MM_PUR_PO_DEFAULT_TEMPLATE)

This template is used for both:

1️⃣  Initial Purchase Order output

2️⃣  Changed Purchase Order output

 

From the system’s perspective, the distinction between a new PO and a changed PO exists - the output messages contain a Change Indicator.

 

However, this distinction is not reflected in the email body sent to suppliers.

As a result, the only indication that a PO has been changed can typically be found inside the attached PDF, not in the email itself.

Why This Is a Problem

From a supplier’s point of view, email notifications are often processed quickly - sometimes automatically, sometimes manually.

When a changed Purchase Order uses the same email wording as a new one, several issues may arise:

  • Suppliers may assume a new order instead of a change
  • Important changes may be overlooked
  • Duplicate deliveries may occur if the “change of” note in the PDF is missed

In short: the lack of a clear distinction in the email text can lead to misinterpretation and operational risks.

The Opportunity for Improvement

The enhancement opportunity is both simple and logical:

Provide a predelivered email template specifically for Changed Purchase Orders.

Since the system already knows whether a message relates to:

  • a new Purchase Order, or
  • a changed Purchase Order (via the Change Indicator),

this information could be used as a trigger to:

  • Select a dedicated predelivered template for PO changes
  • Allow customers to create custom templates based on this distinction

 

This approach would align email output behavior with the actual business context of the message.

Expected Business Benefits

Introducing a specific email template for changed Purchase Orders would deliver immediate value:

✅Clearer supplier communication
Suppliers instantly understand that an existing order has been updated.

✅Greater process stability
Reduced risk of misunderstandings and follow‑up corrections.

✅Prevention of duplicate deliveries
Especially critical when quantity or schedule changes are involved.

✅Improved professionalism of external communication
Emails reflect the real business intent, not just the attached document.

Why This Matters

Email output is often the first and sometimes only touchpoint between SAP and suppliers. If that message is ambiguous, even accurate PDFs may not compensate for the initial confusion.

Given that:

  • SAP already distinguishes between new and changed POs internally, and
  • Business Users can already maintain flexible email templates,

introducing a dedicated predelivered template for PO changes would be a natural and consistent next step.

Sometimes, improving a process does not require new functionality - just a better use of the information already available.

Call to Action

If you agree that Changed Purchase Orders should have their own predelivered email template, 

👉   Please Vote to Idea # 372777 Specific Email Template for Change of Purchase Order on SAP Influence and help move this enhancement forward.

entry.title: Use BAdi implemenetation to update unsupported fields in mass changes to Enterprise Project
entry.content:
Currently, not all of the fields in enterprise projects and WBS Elements are supported with mass change function. BAdi implementation on determination can be used as a workaround.

In this article, let's take WbsIsAvailyControlcontrolobj as an example. This field is not supported in the Fiori App Mass Change to Projects. While it is exposed in the BAdi implementation /S4PPM/PROJECT_DETERMINATION.

 

Here is the BAdi implementation, the check logic will be triggered if the priority code is updated as '003' (Medium). If it is, then the BAdi will update the target field as abap_true.

IF enterpriseproject-prioritycode = '003' and enterpriseproject-wbsisavailycontrolcontrolobj IS INITIAL.
    changedenterpriseproject-wbsisavailycontrolcontrolobj = abap_true.
ENDIF.

 

As this is triggered at the determination stage, so all of the original determination and validation checks will still work as expected.

The trigger field and target field can be replaced by any other fields. But for the trigger field, it is better to be a convertiable field and its update should not trigger any other business scenario.

 

Then schedule a mass change job to update the prioirt code for any enterprise project.

The trigger field priority and the traget field AVC check level are both updated after the mass change job is executed successfully.

 

Links:

Custom Fields App and Custom Logic App 

How to expose more fields in Mass Changes to Projects

entry.title: Solving the Multi-Approval Cause Problem in S/4HANA Cloud Sales Document Workflow
entry.content:
The Problem

If you've set up Flexible Workflow for Sales Documents on SAP S/4HANA Cloud following the Approval Workflows for Sales Documents documentation, you've likely hit this wall: a sales representative modifies a sales order, changing the payment terms, incoterms, and net amount in a single save. Three fields changed. Three potential approval triggers. But only one ApprovalReason is recorded on the document header.

Which one wins? Whichever BAdI logic evaluates last. The others are silently discarded.

The business consequences are real:

  • Lost visibility — approvers see "Payment Terms Changed" but have no idea the price also moved
  • Incorrect routing — different reasons may require different approvers; only one path is activated
  • Audit gaps — compliance cannot trace which changes actually triggered the workflow

How Standard APM Works (Quick Recap)

For those familiar with the Approval Workflows for Sales Documents, the approval flow works like this:

  1. A change is made to the sales order
  2. Cloud BAdI SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON evaluates the change and sets one approval reason code
  3. The system matches that reason to a Workflow Definition (configured in Manage Sales Document Workflows)
  4. A single workflow instance is triggered

The BAdI is the only extension point for determining why approval is needed. But it outputs a single value. If your business has multiple independent approval triggers — price thresholds, incoterms changes, credit checks — you're forced to pick one or create composite reason codes that quickly become unmanageable.

Solution Architecture

The approach separates cause determination (what triggered approval) from reason assignment (what the workflow sees), using the existing BAdI execution sequence:

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              BAdI Execution Sequence                         │
│                                                             │
│  1. SD_SLS_MODIFY_HEAD  → Generate UUID (stable key)       │
│  2. SD_SLS_FINALIZE     → Determine ALL causes,            │
│                            write to table + buffer,         │
│                            display on SO header             │
│  3. SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL → Read causes from buffer,         │
│       _REASON             set single reason for framework  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

The key components:

  • Custom table (ztb_sd_apm_cause) — stores every triggered cause independently, keyed by a UUID
  • Helper class (zcl_sd_apm_cause_writer) — manages an in-memory buffer and DB persistence
  • Display field on the SO header (Key User Extensibility) — shows all causes concatenated (e.g., PAYMENT_TERMS, INCOTERMS, NET_AMOUNT)

The Two-Tier Extensibility Model

This solution bridges Developer Extensibility and Key User Extensibility — a pattern that's essential on S/4HANA Cloud but not always well understood:

Layer What's Created Why

Developer Extensibility (ABAP Cloud, ADT)Custom table ztb_sd_apm_cause, helper class zcl_sd_apm_cause_writerThese are ABAP repository objects that require a developer environment and transport
Release for Key UserTable and class released with C1 contract (USE_IN_KEY_USER_APPS)Cloud BAdIs run in the Key User extensibility layer — they can only call released APIs
Key User Extensibility (Custom Logic app)BAdI implementations for SD_SLS_MODIFY_HEAD, SD_SLS_FINALIZE, SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASONThese are maintained in the Custom Logic app by functional consultants

The critical step: after creating the table and class via Developer Extensibility, you must release them with the appropriate contract so they become callable from Cloud BAdI implementations. Without this release, the Key User BAdI code cannot reference your custom objects — the syntax check will reject them.

See Developing an Extension Using Key User Extensibility for details on the release process.

Why UUID? The Late-Numbering Problem

During sales order creation, the SO number doesn't exist yet when the FINALIZE and APM BAdIs execute (late numbering). We need a stable identifier to link cause records to the order before the number is assigned.

The solution: generate a UUID in MODIFY_HEAD (the earliest BAdI that can write to SO custom fields), store it on the order header, and use it as the foreign key for cause records. Once the SO is saved, both the UUID and the SO number are available for later queries.

Key Design Decisions

Decision Rationale

Separate table instead of SO custom fieldsThe APM BAdI has a read-only contract — it cannot modify SO data. Causes must be stored externally.
UUID as primary key (not SO number)SO number not assigned during creation. UUID is available immediately.
UUID generated in MODIFY_HEADEarliest BAdI in the chain that can write to custom fields.
In-memory buffer in the helper classFINALIZE executes before the APM BAdI within the same LUW. The buffer bridges this gap without requiring a COMMIT.
Cause determination in FINALIZE onlySingle source of truth. The APM BAdI only reads causes and translates to the single reason the framework requires.
Developer Extensibility for table + classCloud BAdI implementations (Key User layer) cannot create tables or complex classes — only call released ones.

Each decision was driven by the BAdI execution order, the read/write contracts of each extension point, and the S/4HANA Cloud extensibility boundaries.

Implementation Highlights

1. Custom Table (Developer Extensibility)

The persistence layer — a simple transparent table keyed by UUID and cause code:

@EndUserText.label : 'Root Causes of Sales Document Approval Reason'
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table ztb_sd_apm_cause {
  key client              : abap.clnt not null;
  key sales_document      : abap.char(10) not null;
  key sales_document_uuid : abap.char(32) not null;
  key cause_code          : abap.char(30) not null;
  created_by              : abap.char(12);
}

2. Helper Class (Developer Extensibility, Released for Key User)

The class manages both an in-memory buffer (for same-LUW access between BAdIs) and database persistence. It's created in ADT and released with C1 - USE_IN_KEY_USER_APPS so Cloud BAdI implementations can call it.

CLASS zcl_sd_apm_cause_writer DEFINITION
  PUBLIC FINAL CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_cause,
             cause_code TYPE c LENGTH 30,
           END OF ty_cause.
    TYPES tt_causes TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_cause WITH EMPTY KEY.

    "! Write causes to buffer + DB. Called from FINALIZE BAdI.
    CLASS-METHODS write
      IMPORTING iv_sales_document_uuid TYPE sysuuid_c32
                iv_sales_document      TYPE vbeln_va
                it_causes              TYPE tt_causes.

    "! Read causes — buffer first (same LUW), DB fallback.
    "! Called from APM BAdI.
    CLASS-METHODS read
      IMPORTING iv_sales_document_uuid TYPE sysuuid_c32
      RETURNING VALUE(rt_causes)       TYPE tt_causes.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_buffer_entry,
             sales_document_uuid TYPE c LENGTH 32,
             cause_code          TYPE c LENGTH 30,
           END OF ty_buffer_entry.
    CLASS-DATA gt_buffer TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_buffer_entry WITH EMPTY KEY.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS zcl_sd_apm_cause_writer IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD read.
    " Priority 1: in-memory buffer (bridges FINALIZE → APM within same LUW)
    rt_causes = VALUE #(
      FOR ls IN gt_buffer
      WHERE ( sales_document_uuid = iv_sales_document_uuid )
      ( cause_code = ls-cause_code ) ).

    IF rt_causes IS NOT INITIAL.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

    " Priority 2: database (for reads after COMMIT, e.g. workflow display)
    SELECT cause_code FROM ztb_sd_apm_cause
      WHERE sales_document_uuid = @iv_sales_document_uuid
      INTO TABLE @rt_causes.
  ENDMETHOD.


  METHOD write.
    " Refresh buffer for this document
    DELETE gt_buffer WHERE sales_document_uuid = iv_sales_document_uuid.
    LOOP AT it_causes INTO DATA(ls_cause).
      APPEND VALUE #( sales_document_uuid = iv_sales_document_uuid
                      cause_code          = ls_cause-cause_code ) TO gt_buffer.
    ENDLOOP.

    " Persist to DB (replace pattern: delete old, insert new)
    DELETE FROM ztb_sd_apm_cause
      WHERE sales_document_uuid = @iv_sales_document_uuid.

    IF it_causes IS INITIAL.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

    INSERT ztb_sd_apm_cause FROM TABLE @( VALUE #(
      FOR ls IN it_causes
      ( sales_document      = iv_sales_document
        sales_document_uuid = iv_sales_document_uuid
        cause_code          = ls-cause_code
        created_by          = cl_abap_context_info=>get_user_technical_name( ) ) ) ).
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

3. BAdI: SD_SLS_MODIFY_HEAD — Generate UUID (Custom Logic App)

The earliest extension point that can write to custom fields. Generates a UUID once per document lifecycle:

" Generate stable UUID for linking cause records to this sales order.
" Required because SO number is not yet assigned during creation (late numbering).
IF salesdocument-yyy1904248uuid_sdh IS INITIAL.
  salesdocument_change-yyy1904248uuid_sdh = cl_system_uuid=>create_uuid_c32_static( ).
  salesdocument_change-control-yyy1904248uuid_sdh = 'X'.
ENDIF.

4. BAdI: SD_SLS_FINALIZE — Determine Causes + Display (Custom Logic App)

The core logic: evaluate which fields changed and persist all triggered causes. Uses the salesdocument_before_image parameter to detect actual changes (not just current values):

DATA lt_causes TYPE zcl_sd_apm_cause_writer=>tt_causes.

" --- Cause: Payment Terms changed to a monitored value ---
IF salesdocument-customerpaymentterms <> salesdocument_before_image-customerpaymentterms
  AND salesdocument-customerpaymentterms = '0007'.
  APPEND VALUE #( cause_code = 'PAYMENT_TERMS' ) TO lt_causes.
ENDIF.

" --- Cause: Incoterms changed ---
IF salesdocument-incotermsclassification <> salesdocument_before_image-incotermsclassification.
  APPEND VALUE #( cause_code = 'INCOTERMS' ) TO lt_causes.
ENDIF.

" --- Cause: Net amount exceeds threshold ---
IF salesdocument-totalnetamount > 10000
  AND salesdocument_before_image-totalnetamount <= 10000.
  APPEND VALUE #( cause_code = 'NET_AMOUNT_THRESHOLD' ) TO lt_causes.
ENDIF.

" Write all causes to buffer + DB
zcl_sd_apm_cause_writer=>write(
  iv_sales_document_uuid = salesdocument-yyy1904248uuid_sdh
  iv_sales_document      = salesdocument-salesdocument
  it_causes              = lt_causes ).

" Display concatenated causes on SO header for approver visibility
IF lt_causes IS NOT INITIAL.
  salesdocument_change-yyy1904248approvalreas_sdh = REDUCE string(
    INIT result = ``
    FOR ls IN lt_causes
    NEXT result = COND #(
      WHEN result IS INITIAL THEN ls-cause_code
      ELSE result && `, ` && ls-cause_code ) ).
ELSE.
  salesdocument_change-yyy1904248approvalreas_sdh = ''.
ENDIF.
salesdocument_change-control-yyy1904248approvalreas_sdh = 'X'.

5. BAdI: SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON — Read + Route (Custom Logic App)

The APM BAdI reads the causes from the buffer and sets the single reason code the framework requires. The routing logic remains simple — the cause table provides the intelligence:

" Read all causes determined in FINALIZE (from buffer, same LUW)
DATA(lt_causes) = zcl_sd_apm_cause_writer=>read(
  salesdocument-yyy1904248uuid_sdh ).

" If any cause was triggered, set approval reason to activate the workflow
IF lt_causes IS NOT INITIAL.
  salesdocapprovalreason = 'ZOR1'.
ENDIF.

" Future enhancement: map specific causes to different reason codes
" for routing to different workflow definitions per cause type.

The end-to-end flow: UUID is generated → FINALIZE detects changes and writes causes → APM BAdI reads causes and triggers the workflow. The approver sees all causes on the order header immediately.

What This Can and Cannot Achieve

Let me be direct about the boundaries.

What works within standard Flexible Workflow:

  • Full visibility of all approval triggers on the sales order
  • Data foundation for multi-level sequential approval — use the Workflow Condition BAdI to dynamically skip or require each step based on the cause table
  • Complete audit trail of what actually triggered approval

What this does NOT deliver:

  • True parallel approval — One SO = one workflow instance. The framework has no fork/join construct. You cannot spawn parallel approval paths for the same document.
  • Multiple simultaneous workflow definitions — Routing is based on one reason → one workflow definition. There's no mechanism to activate multiple definitions simultaneously.

This is not a limitation of the implementation. It's inherent to the S/4HANA Cloud Flexible Workflow engine. The "Manage Sales Document Workflow" app supports only sequential steps (Step 1 → Step 2 → ...).

Future Directions

The cause table establishes a data foundation that both internal and external routing can consume:

  1. Workflow Condition BAdI — query the cause table per step to dynamically decide whether a specific approver is needed. Step 1 handles price changes, Step 2 handles incoterms, Step 3 handles payment terms. Each step checks if its cause is present and skips if not.

  2. SAP Build Process Automation (BPA) — for organizations that need true parallel approval, BPA with Event Mesh can subscribe to the order change event and branch into parallel paths. The cause table provides exactly the routing data BPA needs to decide which branches to activate.

  3. Configuration-driven cause definitions — replace hardcoded checks with a configuration table mapping field changes to cause codes. This enables no-code extensibility for new approval triggers.

The value of building the data layer first: when the framework evolves (or you integrate BPA), the cause data is already there. No rework required.

Conclusion

When your business has multiple independent reasons to approve a sales order, a single ApprovalReason field isn't enough. This approach works within the BAdI execution sequence and the two-tier extensibility model (Developer + Key User) to capture what the standard field cannot, while providing immediate approver visibility.

The total implementation footprint is small: one transparent table, one helper class (both via Developer Extensibility, released for Key User consumption), and three Cloud BAdI implementations. All in ABAP Cloud, all within the S/4HANA Cloud extensibility model.

Prerequisites: This post assumes familiarity with Flexible Workflow setup on S/4HANA Cloud. If you're starting from scratch, see Approval Workflows for Sales Documents and Custom Fields App and Custom Logic App in SAP Help.
